source.access: owner-supplied; editable PPTX not bundled; 17 rendered slide images bundled

rendered_slides.derivation: full-slide renderings from the owner-supplied PPTX

text_only_editorial_adaptation.scope: editable slide text only; no architecture diagram, chart, embedded image, connector, or other visual object was changed
text_only_editorial_adaptation.purpose: replace consciousness-forward terminology with operational language and distinguish the audited run from broader intended architecture

interpretive_source.description: owner-supplied unpublished TeX draft by Yixiao Wang and Aaron Zheng, used for theoretical framing only

owner_attested_chronology.hackathon_phase: The team contributed component research; Eduardo designed and integrated the Global Workspace Theory architecture used in the team presentation.
owner_attested_chronology.post_hackathon_role: Eduardo became the main organizer and took on an informal leadership role, but was never formally designated team leader; Yixiao Wang occasionally advised.
owner_attested_chronology.evaluated_implementation: Eduardo independently implemented and evaluated the integrated architecture within a different but overlapping GenAI at Berkeley project sponsored by Block, Inc.
owner_attested_chronology.summit_credit_context: The six-name title-slide credit preserved the original hackathon-team context because the hackathon win supplied the summit presentation opportunity; it is not a contribution map for the evaluated implementation.

claim_map.claim: The context window serves as a cognitive global workspace managed by a context-sensitive meta-agent controller.
Specialized agents form cognition loops for perception, executive control, belief, imagination, action, memory, and learning.
Conceptual memory records concepts, clusters them semantically, summarizes clusters, and injects those summaries into the next episode.
Episodic memory stores time-ordered belief states by episode and supports later retrieval.

The 139-task evaluation used enhanced planning and active conceptual memory while excluding retrieval of prior episode traces. The run-era code and traces show that a current-game observation history remained active.
A unified latent substrate was presented as future work rather than as part of the evaluated system.

interpretation_constraints: The summit title-slide credit preserves hackathon-team context and must not be interpreted as a contribution map for the evaluated implementation.
The contribution and sponsorship chronology is owner-attested and is not independently established by the deck.
Eduardo must not be described as a formally appointed team leader.

The evaluated runtime retained a current-game observation trace but did not retrieve prior episode transcripts; the deck's shorthand 'episodic memory excluded' refers to the latter cross-game capability.
Run-specific architecture claims must be checked against run_architecture.json rather than inferred from the later summit schematic alone.
The future unified-substrate proposal must not be described as implemented or evaluated.
The W&B evidence bundle remains canonical for success totals and dataset interpretation.
The editable PPTX is not redistributed; the complete 17-slide rendered presentation is bundled.
The text-only editorial adaptation preserves every architecture diagram, chart, embedded image, and connector; the original slide renderings remain bundled separately.
Operational aliases and theoretical interpretation are documented in theory.json and must not be read as renaming the historical runtime in source.
